# Implementation Plan: Modern Coffee Shop Website

**Branch**: `001-coffee-site` | **Date**: 2026-07-14 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)

**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-coffee-site/spec.md`

**Note**: This file is generated by the `/speckit.plan` process and captures the architecture, design decisions, and artifacts for the feature.

## Summary

Build a responsive, polished static coffee shop website using Next.js with static export. The site will include a high-impact landing page, a Menu page with mocked beverage data, and a FAQ page, all rendered as static content with no database or runtime backend.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript / JavaScript with Next.js 15+ (static export mode)

**Primary Dependencies**:
- `next` for static site generation and routing
- `react` / `react-dom` for UI composition
- optional styling library such as `tailwindcss` or CSS modules
- `eslint` / `prettier` for code quality

**Storage**: N/A — content is embedded in static modules or local data files

**Testing**: Browser-based validation via Lighthouse, accessibility audit tools, static link checks, and unit/component tests if added later

**Target Platform**: Modern web browsers on desktop and mobile; deployed as static files on CDN-friendly hosting (Vercel, Netlify, GitHub Pages)

**Project Type**: Static web application (Next.js static export)

**Performance Goals**:
- Page load under 3 seconds on 4G networks
- Lighthouse performance ≥ 85 desktop, ≥ 75 mobile
- Image asset size under 100KB per image after optimization

**Constraints**:
- No backend services, APIs, or databases
- All data is mocked and embedded at build time
- Must satisfy WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility requirements

**Scale/Scope**:
- Small marketing-style website with three primary pages: landing, menu, and FAQ
- Mocked menu dataset with at least 3 beverages, ideally 5+ entries for better presentation
- FAQ content with at least 10 questions

## Constitution Check

The project aligns with the CoffeeSite constitution:
- Static-First Architecture: satisfied through Next.js static export
- Automated Build & Validation: supported by build-time checks and static output
- Performance & Accessibility Standards: addressed with mobile-first responsive design, image optimization, and WCAG goals

No constitution violations are expected for this feature.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-coffee-site/
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
│   ├── faq-data.md
│   ├── menu-data.md
│   └── site-navigation.md
└── spec.md
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
frontend/
├── app/
│   ├── page.tsx
│   ├── menu/page.tsx
│   └── faq/page.tsx
├── components/
│   ├── Header.tsx
│   ├── BeverageCard.tsx
│   ├── FAQSection.tsx
│   └── Footer.tsx
├── data/
│   ├── beverages.ts
│   └── faqs.ts
├── styles/
│   ├── globals.css
│   └── layout.css
└── next.config.mjs

public/
├── images/
│   ├── beverage-espresso.jpg
│   ├── beverage-latte.jpg
│   └── beverage-coldbrew.jpg
```

**Structure Decision**: Use a single Next.js app in `frontend/` with static pages and local data modules. This structure keeps the site self-contained, matches the no-backend requirement, and makes it easy to export the site as static HTML/CSS/JS.

## Complexity Tracking

No additional complexity tracking is required. The design remains aligned with a simple static site and does not introduce architecture violations that need justification.
